title: 2026 Masters Live Updates: Par 3 Contest Results, Leaderboard, Scores and Analysis - CBS Sports
body:
The 90th edition of the Masters Tournament unofficially kicked off at Augusta National Golf Club yesterday with the highly anticipated Par 3 Contest. This cherished tradition, which predates the main event by a day, has been a staple of the Masters experience for decades, offering a unique glimpse into the competitive psyche of the world's top golfers.

As the golfers took to the picturesque 12th hole, affectionately known as the "Golden Bell," for the contest, the atmosphere was electric. The hole, featuring a picturesque pond and a challenging shot over water, has become synonymous with the Par 3 Contest. With a field of 90 competitors, including defending champion Jon Rahm and 2020 champion Dustin Johnson, the stakes may not be as high as they will be come Thursday, but the intensity and focus were palpable.

One of the most intriguing aspects of the Par 3 Contest is the way it allows golfers to gauge their form and fine-tune their skills in a more relaxed environment. Masters champions past and present have long emphasized the importance of this event in shaping their approach to the main tournament. "It's a great way to get our feet wet, so to speak," said Johnson in a pre-tournament interview. "We can get a sense of how our swings are feeling and make any necessary adjustments before Thursday."

For many golfers, the Par 3 Contest serves as a crucial warm-up session, providing an opportunity to shake off any rust and get into a competitive mindset. The event's unique format, which features a mix of short holes and challenging shots, puts a premium on accuracy and strategy. Players must carefully consider their approach and execute flawlessly to card a respectable score, all while navigating the pressure of competing against the world's best.

Another significant advantage of the Par 3 Contest is its ability to create memorable moments and unexpected storylines. In recent years, we've seen surprise winners and dramatic comebacks, which have added an extra layer of excitement to the event. This year's contest was no exception, with several players making bold claims and others struggling to find their footing.

As the golfers prepare to tee off tomorrow, they'll be carrying the lessons learned from yesterday's Par 3 Contest into the main tournament. The event's impact may not be immediately apparent, but its influence can be felt throughout the week. With a field as talented as the one assembled at Augusta National, anything can happen. The stage is set for an unforgettable Masters Tournament, and the Par 3 Contest has provided a tantalizing preview of what's to come.

As the world watches the greatest golfers in action, one thing is certain: the Masters Tournament will be a true test of skill, strategy, and mental toughness. The Par 3 Contest may have unofficially kicked off the event, but it's the main tournament that will ultimately decide the champion.
